desc: Termos, processos e conceitos do Project Management Body of Knowledge (PMBOK Guide).

cards.def: A document issued by the project sponsor that formally authorizes the existence of a project and provides the project manager with the authority to apply organizational resources.
cards.ex: The project charter was signed by the CEO, giving the green light to the new product development.
cards.tip: O project charter é o primeiro documento oficial do projeto e deve conter objetivos, principais stakeholders e orçamento preliminar.

cards.def: A formal, approved document that defines how the project is executed, monitored, and controlled.
cards.ex: The project management plan integrated the scope, schedule, and cost baselines.
cards.tip: É um documento composto por vários planos auxiliares (escopo, cronograma, riscos, etc.) e linhas de base.

cards.def: A hierarchical decomposition of the total scope of work to be carried out by the project team.
cards.ex: The WBS broke the project down into 25 work packages, each assigned to a team member.
cards.tip: A regra 100% é fundamental: a EAP deve incluir todo o trabalho definido no escopo, nem mais, nem menos.

cards.def: The lowest level of the work breakdown structure, where work can be estimated, scheduled, and managed.
cards.ex: Each work package was estimated in hours and assigned to a specific department.
cards.tip: Pacotes de trabalho são a base para o cronograma, orçamento e alocação de recursos.

cards.def: A detailed description of the project scope, including major deliverables, assumptions, and constraints.
cards.ex: The scope statement clarified that the software would be delivered only for iOS initially.
cards.tip: A declaração de escopo serve como base para decisões futuras e para evitar scope creep.

cards.def: Uncontrolled changes or continuous growth in a project’s scope, without adjustments to time, cost, or resources.
cards.ex: Scope creep turned a three-month project into a one-year nightmare.
cards.tip: Evite scope creep com um processo formal de controle de mudanças.

cards.def: A formally chartered group responsible for reviewing, evaluating, approving, or rejecting changes to the project.
cards.ex: The CCB met weekly to assess change requests and their impact on the baseline.
cards.tip: A CCB geralmente é formada por stakeholders-chave e tem autoridade para aprovar ou rejeitar mudanças.

cards.def: A formal proposal to modify any document, deliverable, or baseline.
cards.ex: The client submitted a change request to add a new feature to the mobile app.
cards.tip: Toda mudança deve ser documentada e avaliada quanto ao impacto em custo, prazo e qualidade.

cards.def: An intentional activity that realigns the performance of the project work with the project management plan.
cards.ex: The team implemented corrective action by adding two developers to meet the deadline.
cards.tip: Ação corretiva é diferente de ação preventiva: corrige um desvio já ocorrido.

cards.def: An intentional activity that ensures the future performance of the project work is aligned with the project management plan.
cards.ex: Preventive action was taken by conducting extra tests to avoid potential failures.
cards.tip: Ação preventiva visa reduzir a probabilidade de impactos negativos.

cards.def: An activity to fix a nonconformance or defect found in a deliverable.
cards.ex: Defect repair involved rewriting the faulty module to meet the specification.
cards.tip: Defeitos devem ser registrados e tratados como parte do controle de qualidade.

cards.def: The knowledge gained during a project, which shows how project events were addressed or should be addressed for future projects.
cards.ex: The lessons learned session highlighted the importance of early stakeholder engagement.
cards.tip: Lições aprendidas devem ser documentadas ao longo do projeto, não apenas no final.

cards.def: Data and documents from previous projects used to inform current project decisions.
cards.ex: The team used historical information from similar projects to estimate costs more accurately.
cards.tip: Informações históricas são valiosas para estimativas e planejamento.

cards.def: Plans, processes, policies, procedures, and knowledge bases specific to the performing organization.
cards.ex: The project followed the organizational process assets, including the standard risk template.
cards.tip: Ativos de processos organizacionais podem ser processos formais ou informais, como templates e lições aprendidas.

cards.def: Conditions, not under the immediate control of the team, that influence, constrain, or direct the project.
cards.ex: Government regulations were enterprise environmental factors that affected the project timeline.
cards.tip: Exemplos: cultura organizacional, infraestrutura, condições de mercado, regulamentações.

cards.def: An individual, group, or organization that may affect, be affected by, or perceive itself to be affected by a project.
cards.ex: Key stakeholders included the sponsor, the customer, and the regulatory agency.
cards.tip: Identificar stakeholders cedo é crucial para o sucesso do projeto.

cards.def: A document containing the identification, assessment, and classification of project stakeholders.
cards.ex: The stakeholder register listed each stakeholder's influence, interest, and communication preferences.
cards.tip: O registro de stakeholders é atualizado ao longo do projeto.

cards.def: A technique of systematically gathering and analyzing quantitative and qualitative information to determine whose interests should be taken into account.
cards.ex: Stakeholder analysis revealed that the operations manager had high influence but low interest.
cards.tip: Ferramentas comuns: matriz poder/interesse, modelo de saliência.

cards.def: The series of phases that a project passes through from its start to its completion.
cards.ex: The project life cycle included initiation, planning, execution, and closure phases.
cards.tip: O ciclo de vida pode ser preditivo, iterativo, incremental ou ágil.

cards.def: A review at the end of a phase in which a decision is made to continue, modify, or end a project.
cards.ex: At the phase gate, the steering committee approved moving to the construction phase.
cards.tip: Também chamado de stage gate ou kill point.

cards.def: A person or group who provides resources and support for the project and is accountable for enabling success.
cards.ex: The project sponsor secured the budget and helped remove organizational obstacles.
cards.tip: O sponsor é diferente do gerente de projetos; ele tem autoridade executiva.

cards.def: The person assigned by the performing organization to lead the team and achieve the project objectives.
cards.ex: The project manager coordinated the team, managed risks, and reported progress to the sponsor.
cards.tip: O gerente de projetos integra as áreas de conhecimento e lidera a equipe.

cards.def: A set of individuals who support the project manager in performing the work of the project.
cards.ex: The project team consisted of developers, testers, and a business analyst.
cards.tip: A equipe pode ser interna, terceirizada ou virtual.

cards.def: Someone with management authority over an organizational unit, such as a department.
cards.ex: The functional manager provided resources and technical guidance to the project team.
cards.tip: Em organizações matriciais, o functional manager compartilha autoridade com o gerente de projetos.

cards.def: An organizational structure that standardizes project-related governance processes and facilitates the sharing of resources, methodologies, tools, and techniques.
cards.ex: The PMO provided templates and conducted audits to ensure compliance with the methodology.
cards.tip: PMOs podem ser de suporte, controle ou diretivo.

cards.def: A documented economic feasibility study used to justify the investment and authorize the project.
cards.ex: The business case showed a net present value of $2M, leading to project approval.
cards.tip: O business case é criado antes do project charter e ajuda a decidir se o projeto vale a pena.

cards.def: A document that describes how and when the benefits of the project will be delivered and measured.
cards.ex: The benefits management plan outlined key performance indicators for the new system.
cards.tip: Os benefícios devem ser mensuráveis e alinhados aos objetivos estratégicos.

cards.def: Conditions or capabilities that must be met by the project or present in the product, service, or result.
cards.ex: The requirements included both functional and non-functional specifications.
cards.tip: Requisitos bem definidos reduzem retrabalho e aumentam a satisfação do cliente.

cards.def: A grid that links requirements to their origin and traces them throughout the project life cycle.
cards.ex: The traceability matrix ensured that every requirement was tested and delivered.
cards.tip: Ajuda a garantir que todos os requisitos sejam atendidos e facilita o impacto de mudanças.

cards.def: The approved version of the scope statement, WBS, and WBS dictionary, used as a basis for comparison.
cards.ex: Any change to the scope baseline required formal approval through the change control process.
cards.tip: A linha de base do escopo é parte do plano de gerenciamento do projeto.

cards.def: A document that provides detailed deliverable, activity, and scheduling information about each component in the WBS.
cards.ex: The WBS dictionary described each work package, its cost, and acceptance criteria.
cards.tip: Complementa a EAP com informações detalhadas.

cards.def: A distinct, scheduled portion of work performed during the course of a project.
cards.ex: The activity 'code review' was estimated to take four hours.
cards.tip: Atividades são os menores componentes do cronograma.

cards.def: A documented tabulation of schedule activities that includes the activity name, identifier, and description.
cards.ex: The activity list contained 150 tasks, each with a unique ID.
cards.tip: A lista de atividades é uma saída do processo de definição das atividades.

cards.def: Multiple attributes associated with each schedule activity, such as predecessors, resources, and constraints.
cards.ex: Activity attributes included the required skill level and the earliest start date.
cards.tip: Os atributos se expandem à medida que o planejamento avança.

cards.def: A significant point or event in a project, usually with zero duration.
cards.ex: The project milestone 'design approval' was achieved on March 15th.
cards.tip: Marcos ajudam a monitorar o progresso e a comunicar eventos importantes.

cards.def: An activity that logically comes before a dependent activity in a schedule.
cards.ex: The predecessor of 'testing' was 'development'.
cards.tip: As relações entre atividades podem ser finish-to-start, start-to-start, etc.

cards.def: An activity that logically follows another activity in a schedule.
cards.ex: 'Deployment' was the successor to 'testing'.
cards.tip: Identificar sucessores ajuda a construir a rede do cronograma.

cards.def: A modification of a logical relationship that directs a delay in the successor activity.
cards.ex: A lag of two days was added after painting to allow the paint to dry.
cards.tip: Lag é um tempo de espera; lead é uma antecipação.

cards.def: A modification of a logical relationship that allows an acceleration of the successor activity.
cards.ex: A lead of one week allowed the procurement team to start ordering materials before the design was fully complete.
cards.tip: Lead acelera o cronograma, mas aumenta riscos.

cards.def: The sequence of activities that represents the longest path through a project, determining the shortest possible duration.
cards.ex: Any delay on the critical path would directly push the project end date.
cards.tip: O caminho crítico tem folga zero ou negativa.

cards.def: The amount of time an activity can be delayed without delaying the project finish date.
cards.ex: The activity had five days of float, so it was not on the critical path.
cards.tip: Float total e float livre são conceitos importantes na análise de rede.

cards.def: The approved version of the project schedule, used as a basis for comparison.
cards.ex: The schedule baseline was established after the planning phase.
cards.tip: Mudanças no cronograma devem ser aprovadas e a linha de base atualizada.

cards.def: Techniques used to shorten the project duration without reducing the project scope.
cards.ex: Crashing and fast tracking are two common schedule compression techniques.
cards.tip: Crashing adiciona recursos; fast tracking executa atividades em paralelo.

cards.def: A technique for shortening the schedule by adding resources, typically at a higher cost.
cards.ex: Crashing involved adding two more developers to the coding phase.
cards.tip: Crashing aumenta custo, mas pode reduzir prazo.

cards.def: A technique for shortening the schedule by performing activities in parallel that would normally be done sequentially.
cards.ex: Fast tracking allowed design and development to overlap, saving two months.
cards.tip: Fast tracking aumenta risco de retrabalho.

cards.def: The approved version of the time-phased project budget, used for comparison and control.
cards.ex: The cost baseline included all planned expenditures over the project life.
cards.tip: A linha de base de custos exclui reservas de contingência.

cards.def: Time or budget allocated for identified risks that are accepted.
cards.ex: The project had a 10% contingency reserve for unexpected cost overruns.
cards.tip: A reserva de contingência faz parte da linha de base e é gerenciada pelo gerente de projetos.

cards.def: An amount of the project budget withheld for management control purposes for unforeseen work that is within scope.
cards.ex: The management reserve was only accessible with sponsor approval.
cards.tip: A reserva de gerenciamento não faz parte da linha de base.

cards.def: A quantitative assessment of the likely costs of the resources required to complete project activities.
cards.ex: The cost estimate was developed using analogous estimating based on past projects.
cards.tip: Existem vários níveis de estimativa: ordem de grandeza, orçamento, definitiva.

cards.def: A technique for estimating the duration or cost of an activity using historical data from a similar activity.
cards.ex: Using analogous estimating, the team predicted the project would take six months.
cards.tip: É uma técnica de estimativa rápida, mas menos precisa.

cards.def: A technique that uses a statistical relationship between historical data and other variables to calculate an estimate.
cards.ex: Parametric estimating calculated the cost as $100 per square meter, totaling $500k.
cards.tip: A precisão depende da qualidade dos dados e da relação paramétrica.

cards.def: A method of estimating project duration or cost by aggregating the estimates of the lower-level components.
cards.ex: Bottom-up estimating started with each work package and rolled up to the total project cost.
cards.tip: É a técnica mais precisa, mas também a mais demorada.

cards.def: A technique that uses optimistic, pessimistic, and most likely estimates to improve accuracy.
cards.ex: Three-point estimating gave a weighted average duration of 12 days.
cards.tip: A fórmula triangular (P+M+O)/3 ou PERT (P+4M+O)/6.

cards.def: A methodology that combines scope, schedule, and resource measurements to assess project performance.
cards.ex: EVM showed that the project was behind schedule but under budget.
cards.tip: Indicadores-chave: PV, EV, AC, CV, SV, CPI, SPI.

cards.def: The authorized budget assigned to scheduled work.
cards.ex: The planned value at month three was $200,000.
cards.tip: PV é a linha de base do orçamento até a data.

cards.def: The measure of work performed expressed in terms of the budget authorized for that work.
cards.ex: The earned value at this point is $180,000, meaning 90% of planned work is done.
cards.tip: EV = % concluído × orçamento total.

cards.def: The realized cost incurred for the work performed on an activity during a specific time period.
cards.ex: The actual cost so far is $190,000.
cards.tip: AC é o custo efetivamente gasto.

cards.def: The difference between earned value and actual cost (CV = EV – AC).
cards.ex: CV = –$10,000 indicates the project is over budget.
cards.tip: CV negativo = acima do orçamento; positivo = abaixo.

cards.def: The difference between earned value and planned value (SV = EV – PV).
cards.ex: SV = –$20,000 means the project is behind schedule.
cards.tip: SV negativo = atrasado; positivo = adiantado.

cards.def: A measure of cost efficiency (CPI = EV / AC).
cards.ex: CPI = 0.95 means for every $1 spent, only $0.95 of work was earned.
cards.tip: CPI < 1 = acima do orçamento; > 1 = abaixo.

cards.def: A measure of schedule efficiency (SPI = EV / PV).
cards.ex: SPI = 0.9 indicates the project is 10% behind schedule.
cards.tip: SPI < 1 = atrasado; > 1 = adiantado.

cards.def: The expected total cost of completing all work, expressed as the sum of actual cost and estimate to complete.
cards.ex: EAC = AC + (BAC – EV) / CPI, projecting a final cost of $1.1M.
cards.tip: Várias fórmulas de EAC dependem das condições do projeto.

cards.def: The expected cost to finish all the remaining project work.
cards.ex: ETC = EAC – AC, or a new bottom-up estimate.
cards.tip: ETC pode ser calculado de diferentes formas.

cards.def: The projected budget surplus or deficit (VAC = BAC – EAC).
cards.ex: VAC = –$50,000 means the project is expected to be $50k over budget.
cards.tip: VAC negativo = acima do orçamento.

cards.def: The cost performance needed to achieve a specific management goal, such as BAC or EAC.
cards.ex: TCPI = (BAC – EV) / (BAC – AC) = 1.2, requiring tight cost control.
cards.tip: TCPI > 1 é difícil de alcançar.

cards.def: The degree to which a set of inherent characteristics fulfills requirements.
cards.ex: Quality was measured by the number of defects per module.
cards.tip: Qualidade não é apenas ausência de defeitos, mas atender às necessidades.

cards.def: A category assigned to products or services having the same functional use but different technical characteristics.
cards.ex: The software was delivered in three grades: basic, professional, and enterprise.
cards.tip: Baixa qualidade é sempre um problema; baixo grau pode ser aceitável.

cards.def: The closeness of repeated measurements to each other.
cards.ex: The measurements had high precision but low accuracy.
cards.tip: Precisão ≠ exatidão (accuracy).

cards.def: The closeness of measured values to the true value.
cards.ex: The instrument provided accurate readings within 0.1%.
cards.tip: Exatidão é o alvo; precisão é a consistência.

cards.def: All costs incurred over the life of the product to prevent poor quality or to correct defects.
cards.ex: COQ included training, testing, and rework expenses.
cards.tip: Divide-se em custos de conformidade (prevenção, avaliação) e não conformidade (falhas internas, externas).

cards.def: Costs incurred to prevent defects and keep quality high, such as training and process documentation.
cards.ex: Prevention costs included quality training for the team.
cards.tip: Investir em prevenção reduz falhas futuras.

cards.def: Costs incurred to assess quality, such as testing and inspections.
cards.ex: Appraisal costs covered the third-party audit.
cards.tip: Avaliação detecta defeitos, mas não os previne.

cards.def: Costs incurred to correct defects, divided into internal and external failure costs.
cards.ex: Rework was a major failure cost in the project.
cards.tip: Falhas externas (após entrega) são mais caras que internas.

cards.def: A component of the project management plan that describes how quality will be managed and controlled.
cards.ex: The quality management plan specified peer reviews and testing phases.
cards.tip: Inclui métricas, papéis e responsabilidades.

cards.def: A description of a project or product attribute and how to measure it.
cards.ex: Quality metrics included defect density and customer satisfaction score.
cards.tip: As métricas devem ser SMART (específicas, mensuráveis, etc.).

cards.def: A structured, independent process to determine if project activities comply with organizational and project policies.
cards.ex: The quality audit identified gaps in the testing process.
cards.tip: Auditorias podem ser internas ou externas.

cards.def: A technique to identify opportunities for process improvements.
cards.ex: Process analysis revealed bottlenecks in the approval workflow.
cards.tip: Utiliza ferramentas como diagramas de fluxo e value stream mapping.

cards.def: Graphical displays of data over time with predetermined control limits.
cards.ex: The control chart showed that the defect rate was within acceptable limits.
cards.tip: Usados para monitorar a estabilidade de um processo.

cards.def: A visual tool that lists potential causes of a problem, often called fishbone diagram.
cards.ex: The team used a cause-and-effect diagram to explore reasons for delays.
cards.tip: Ajuda a encontrar causas raiz de problemas.

cards.def: A diagram that shows the steps in a process and their relationships.
cards.ex: The flowchart illustrated the software deployment process.
cards.tip: Útil para entender e melhorar processos.

cards.def: A bar chart that shows the distribution of data over categories.
cards.ex: The histogram showed that most delays occurred in the testing phase.
cards.tip: Também chamado de diagrama de distribuição de frequências.

cards.def: A histogram, ordered by frequency, that shows which factors are most significant.
cards.ex: The Pareto chart revealed that 80% of defects came from 20% of modules.
cards.tip: Baseado no princípio 80/20.

cards.def: A graph that shows the relationship between two variables.
cards.ex: The scatter diagram suggested a correlation between team size and productivity.
cards.tip: Ajuda a identificar tendências e correlações.

cards.def: Choosing a part of a population of interest for inspection.
cards.ex: Statistical sampling was used to test 10% of the deliverables.
cards.tip: Reduz custo e tempo de inspeção.

cards.def: The processes that organize, manage, and lead the project team.
cards.ex: Human resource management included team building and performance reviews.
cards.tip: Hoje parte do gerenciamento de recursos (pessoas).

cards.def: A graphical display of project team members and their reporting relationships.
cards.ex: The project organizational chart showed the project manager reporting to the sponsor.
cards.tip: Pode incluir funções e responsabilidades.

cards.def: A grid that shows the project resources assigned to each work package.
cards.ex: The RAM, often a RACI matrix, clarified who was accountable for each task.
cards.tip: RACI = Responsible, Accountable, Consulted, Informed.

cards.def: A common type of RAM that uses the categories Responsible, Accountable, Consulted, and Informed.
cards.ex: The RACI matrix eliminated confusion about who approved changes.
cards.tip: A = Accountable (apenas um por tarefa).

cards.def: A document that describes how and when human resources will be acquired and released.
cards.ex: The staffing management plan included a timeline for hiring contractors.
cards.tip: Pode incluir treinamento e reconhecimento.

cards.def: A document that establishes the team values, agreements, and operating guidelines.
cards.ex: The team charter defined communication norms and meeting schedules.
cards.tip: Ajuda a alinhar a equipe desde o início.

cards.def: The process of handling disagreements within the project team or with stakeholders.
cards.ex: Conflict management techniques included collaborating and compromising.
cards.tip: Cinco modos: competir, colaborar, comprometer, evitar, acomodar.

cards.def: Concepts like Maslow's hierarchy, Herzberg's theory, and McGregor's XY.
cards.ex: Applying motivation theories helped improve team morale.
cards.tip: Útil para entender o que engaja a equipe.

cards.def: The processes to ensure timely and appropriate generation, collection, distribution, and storage of project information.
cards.ex: Effective communications management avoided misunderstandings.
cards.tip: Plano de comunicações define quem, o quê, quando e como.

cards.def: A document that describes how project communications will be planned, structured, and monitored.
cards.ex: The communications plan specified weekly status reports and monthly steering meetings.
cards.tip: Inclui formato, frequência e responsáveis.

cards.def: The number of potential communication paths in a project, calculated as n(n–1)/2.
cards.ex: With 10 stakeholders, there are 45 communication channels.
cards.tip: Mais stakeholders = mais complexidade de comunicação.

cards.def: Information sent to recipients, such as emails, memos, and reports.
cards.ex: Push communication was used for weekly status updates.
cards.tip: Garante que a informação foi enviada, mas não que foi recebida.

cards.def: Information accessed by recipients at their own discretion, like intranet or shared drives.
cards.ex: Project documents were available via pull communication on the SharePoint site.
cards.tip: Útil para grandes volumes de informação.

cards.def: Multi-directional exchange of information in real time, such as meetings and calls.
cards.ex: Interactive communication in the daily stand-up resolved issues quickly.
cards.tip: Mais eficaz para esclarecer dúvidas.

cards.def: An uncertain event or condition that, if it occurs, has a positive or negative effect on one or more project objectives.
cards.ex: The risk of a supplier delay was identified early.
cards.tip: Riscos podem ser ameaças (negativos) ou oportunidades (positivos).

cards.def: A document describing how risk management will be structured and performed.
cards.ex: The risk management plan defined the risk categories and probability-impact matrix.
cards.tip: Inclui metodologia, papéis, orçamento e frequência.

cards.def: A document in which the results of risk analysis and response planning are recorded.
cards.ex: The risk register listed each risk, its probability, impact, and owner.
cards.tip: Atualizado ao longo do projeto.

cards.def: A document presenting the overall risk exposure and key risk information.
cards.ex: The risk report highlighted the top three risks for executive attention.
cards.tip: Pode incluir tendências e análise de reservas.

cards.def: The degree of uncertainty an organization is willing to accept in anticipation of a reward.
cards.ex: The organization had a high risk appetite for innovative projects.
cards.tip: Diferente de tolerância e limiar de risco.

cards.def: The level of risk that an organization can withstand.
cards.ex: The risk tolerance for cost overrun was 10% of the budget.
cards.tip: É a faixa aceitável de variação.

cards.def: The level of risk exposure above which risks are addressed and below which they may be accepted.
cards.ex: Any risk with impact > $50k required a response plan.
cards.tip: Acima do limiar, ação é necessária.

cards.def: The process of determining which risks may affect the project and documenting their characteristics.
cards.ex: Brainstorming sessions were used for risk identification.
cards.tip: Envolve a equipe e stakeholders.

cards.def: A group of potential causes of risk, such as technical, external, organizational, or project management.
cards.ex: Risk categories were defined in the risk breakdown structure (RBS).
cards.tip: RBS é uma estrutura hierárquica de categorias.

cards.def: A hierarchical representation of risks according to their categories.
cards.ex: The RBS helped the team think of risks in a structured way.
cards.tip: Similar à WBS, mas para riscos.

cards.def: The process of prioritizing individual risks for further analysis or action by assessing their probability and impact.
cards.ex: Qualitative analysis classified risks as high, medium, or low.
cards.tip: Utiliza matriz probabilidade-impacto.

cards.def: A grid for ranking risks based on their probability of occurrence and impact on objectives.
cards.ex: The matrix showed which risks needed immediate response.
cards.tip: Cores (vermelho, amarelo, verde) indicam prioridade.

cards.def: The process of numerically analyzing the effect of identified risks on overall project objectives.
cards.ex: Monte Carlo simulation was used for quantitative risk analysis.
cards.tip: Mais usada em projetos grandes e complexos.

cards.def: A statistical technique that calculates the average outcome when the future includes scenarios that may or may not happen.
cards.ex: EMV = probability × impact, used in decision tree analysis.
cards.tip: Útil para comparar opções.

cards.def: A diagram that shows the implications of choosing one alternative over another.
cards.ex: The decision tree helped choose between building or buying the software.
cards.tip: Incorpora probabilidades e EMV.

cards.def: A technique to determine which risks have the most potential impact on the project.
cards.ex: Tornado diagrams are often used in sensitivity analysis.
cards.tip: Ajuda a focar nos riscos mais críticos.

cards.def: A technique that computes the distribution of possible project outcomes by running multiple trials.
cards.ex: Monte Carlo simulation showed a 75% chance of finishing on budget.
cards.tip: Usada para avaliar prazos e custos.

cards.def: The process of developing options and actions to enhance opportunities and reduce threats.
cards.ex: Risk response planning included mitigation strategies for top risks.
cards.tip: Envolve estratégias para ameaças e oportunidades.

cards.def: A risk response strategy for threats where the project team acts to eliminate the threat or protect the project from its impact.
cards.ex: The team avoided the risk by using a proven technology instead of an experimental one.
cards.tip: Elimina a incerteza.

cards.def: A risk response strategy for threats where the team acts to reduce the probability or impact of a risk.
cards.ex: Additional testing mitigated the risk of software defects.
cards.tip: Reduz, mas não elimina completamente.

cards.def: A risk response strategy for threats where the team shifts the impact to a third party.
cards.ex: The project transferred the financial risk by purchasing insurance.
cards.tip: Não elimina o risco, apenas transfere a responsabilidade.

cards.def: A risk response strategy where the team acknowledges the risk and takes no action unless it occurs.
cards.ex: The team accepted the low-impact risk and set aside a contingency reserve.
cards.tip: Aceitação pode ser ativa (reserva) ou passiva.

cards.def: A risk response strategy for opportunities where the team acts to ensure the opportunity is realized.
cards.ex: The team exploited the opportunity by assigning top developers to accelerate delivery.
cards.tip: Usado para oportunidades de alto impacto.

cards.def: A risk response strategy for opportunities where the team acts to increase the probability or impact of an opportunity.
cards.ex: The team enhanced the opportunity by adding more features.
cards.tip: Aumenta os benefícios potenciais.

cards.def: A risk response strategy for opportunities where the team allocates ownership to a third party best able to capture the opportunity.
cards.ex: The project shared the opportunity by partnering with a specialist firm.
cards.tip: Parcerias são comuns.

cards.def: The processes necessary to purchase or acquire products, services, or results from outside the project team.
cards.ex: Procurement management included vendor selection and contract administration.
cards.tip: Envolve contratos e relacionamento com fornecedores.

cards.def: A document that describes how procurement processes will be managed.
cards.ex: The procurement plan defined the bidding process and evaluation criteria.
cards.tip: Define tipos de contrato, prazos e responsáveis.

cards.def: A description of the procurement item sufficient to allow prospective sellers to determine if they can meet the requirement.
cards.ex: The SOW detailed the technical specifications for the software to be purchased.
cards.tip: Parte do pacote de licitação.

cards.def: The set of attributes used to select a seller, such as price, technical capability, and past performance.
cards.ex: Source selection criteria included cost, quality, and delivery time.
cards.tip: Podem ser objetivos ou subjetivos.

cards.def: A technique used to determine whether a particular product or service should be produced internally or purchased.
cards.ex: Make-or-buy analysis concluded that outsourcing was cheaper.
cards.tip: Considera custos, capacidade e riscos.

cards.def: Categories of contracts, such as fixed-price, cost-reimbursable, and time and materials.
cards.ex: A fixed-price contract transferred cost risk to the seller.
cards.tip: Cada tipo aloca riscos de forma diferente.

cards.def: A contract where the price is set and not subject to change unless scope changes.
cards.ex: The fixed-price contract protected the buyer from cost overruns.
cards.tip: Também chamado lump sum.

cards.def: A contract where the seller is paid for actual costs plus a fee.
cards.ex: Cost-reimbursable contracts are used when scope is uncertain.
cards.tip: Pode incluir incentivos.

cards.def: A hybrid contract that includes aspects of both fixed-price and cost-reimbursable.
cards.ex: Time and materials contracts are common for consulting services.
cards.tip: Usado quando não é possível estimar o esforço.

cards.def: A document used to solicit proposals from prospective sellers.
cards.ex: The RFP was sent to five vendors, asking for detailed solutions and pricing.
cards.tip: RFI (informação), RFP (proposta), RFQ (cotação).

cards.def: A document used to solicit price quotations from sellers.
cards.ex: The RFQ requested unit prices for 1000 units.
cards.tip: Mais comum para produtos padronizados.

cards.def: The process of reviewing seller proposals against the criteria.
cards.ex: Proposal evaluation used a weighted scoring system.
cards.tip: Pode envolver equipe multidisciplinar.

cards.def: The process of reaching an agreement between buyer and seller on contract terms.
cards.ex: Negotiation focused on price, delivery schedule, and warranties.
cards.tip: Deve ser ganha-ganha.

cards.pt: sistema de controle de mudanças em contratos
cards.def: The process for modifying contracts, including paperwork and approval levels.
cards.ex: Any contract change required a signed change order.
cards.tip: Protege ambas as partes.

cards.def: Documents used in procurement activities, such as RFPs, RFQs, and contracts.
cards.ex: All procurement documents were stored in a shared repository.
cards.tip: Devem ser claros e completos.

cards.def: A narrative description of the work required for the project or procurement.
cards.ex: The SOW outlined the deliverables and acceptance criteria.
cards.tip: Pode ser para projeto inteiro ou para um contrato.

cards.def: Raw observations and measurements identified during activities.
cards.ex: Work performance data included hours worked and units completed.
cards.tip: É a base para informações de desempenho.

cards.def: Performance data analyzed to provide context, such as variance analysis.
cards.ex: Work performance information showed that the project was 10% behind.
cards.tip: Usado para relatórios.

cards.def: Physical or electronic representations of work performance information compiled to facilitate decision-making.
cards.ex: The monthly report included SPI, CPI, and status updates.
cards.tip: Destinado a stakeholders.

cards.def: A document where project issues are recorded and tracked.
cards.ex: The issue log tracked each problem, its owner, and resolution date.
cards.tip: Diferente de risco: issue já ocorreu.

cards.def: A document listing all change requests and their status.
cards.ex: The change log showed that three changes were approved this month.
cards.tip: Ajuda a rastrear o histórico.

cards.def: A document recording key decisions made during the project.
cards.ex: The decision log included the rationale for choosing the cloud provider.
cards.tip: Útil para auditoria e lições aprendidas.

cards.def: A document where assumptions and constraints are recorded throughout the project.
cards.ex: The assumption log noted that the exchange rate would remain stable.
cards.tip: Premissas devem ser validadas.

cards.def: A limiting factor that affects the execution of a project, such as budget, time, or resources.
cards.ex: The fixed deadline was a major constraint.
cards.tip: Restrições são conhecidas desde o início.

cards.def: A factor that, for planning purposes, is considered to be true, real, or certain.
cards.ex: The assumption that key personnel would be available proved false.
cards.tip: Premissas envolvem risco.

cards.def: The processes and activities to identify, define, combine, unify, and coordinate the various project management processes.
cards.ex: Integration management ensured all parts of the plan worked together.
cards.tip: É a área que une todas as outras.

cards.def: Techniques used to evaluate and choose among projects, such as NPV, IRR, payback period.
cards.ex: NPV was the primary project selection method.
cards.tip: Ajuda a priorizar projetos.

cards.def: The sum of the present values of cash flows over time, minus the initial investment.
cards.ex: A positive NPV of $500k justified the project.
cards.tip: NPV > 0 significa que o projeto agrega valor.

cards.def: The discount rate at which the NPV of cash flows equals zero.
cards.ex: The IRR of 15% exceeded the company's hurdle rate.
cards.tip: Quanto maior, melhor.

cards.def: The time required to recover the investment.
cards.ex: The payback period was estimated at 3 years.
cards.tip: Não considera o valor do dinheiro no tempo.

cards.def: The ratio of benefits to costs.
cards.ex: A BCR of 2.5 means for every $1 spent, $2.5 are returned.
cards.tip: BCR > 1 é favorável.

cards.def: The value of the forgone alternative when a decision is made.
cards.ex: Choosing project A means the opportunity cost is the benefit of project B.
cards.tip: Importante na seleção de projetos.

cards.def: Money already spent that cannot be recovered.
cards.ex: Sunk costs should not influence future decisions.
cards.tip: Evite a falácia do custo irrecuperável.

cards.def: Adding more resources does not always increase output proportionally.
cards.ex: Adding a third developer to a two-person team may not double productivity.
cards.tip: Lembre-se ao fazer crashing.

cards.def: Current assets minus current liabilities; used for day-to-day operations.
cards.ex: The project needed working capital to pay suppliers.
cards.tip: Relevante em projetos internos.

cards.def: The decrease in value of an asset over time.
cards.ex: The equipment was depreciated over five years.
cards.tip: Pode ser linear ou acelerada.

cards.def: The gradual write-off of an intangible asset.
cards.ex: Software development costs were amortized over three years.
cards.tip: Diferente de depreciação (tangíveis).

cards.pt: sistema de informações de gerenciamento de projetos
cards.def: A system that provides information to support project management, such as scheduling software.
cards.ex: The PMIS was used to track progress and generate reports.
cards.tip: Pode ser uma ferramenta ou conjunto delas.

cards.def: A system for managing and controlling versions of project documents and deliverables.
cards.ex: The configuration management system ensured the latest design was used.
cards.tip: Inclui controle de versão.

cards.def: A set of procedures that describes how changes to project deliverables and documents are managed.
cards.ex: The change control system required a change request form and CCB approval.
cards.tip: Parte do plano de gerenciamento.

cards.def: The management of changes to documents, files, or code.
cards.ex: Version control prevented accidental overwrites.
cards.tip: Ferramentas como Git são comuns.

cards.def: The process of storing, tracking, and retrieving project documents.
cards.ex: A document management system organized all project files.
cards.tip: Inclui nomeação, versionamento e acesso.

cards.def: The process of creating, sharing, and using knowledge within an organization.
cards.ex: Lessons learned databases are a form of knowledge management.
cards.tip: Ajuda a reter aprendizado.

cards.def: A document where lessons learned are recorded throughout the project.
cards.ex: The lessons learned register was updated after each phase.
cards.tip: Alimenta a base de conhecimento.

cards.def: A review conducted after project completion to evaluate success and capture lessons.
cards.ex: The post-project review highlighted what went well and what could be improved.
cards.tip: Também chamado de post-mortem.

cards.def: The process of finalizing all activities and formally closing the project.
cards.ex: Project closure involved handing over deliverables and releasing resources.
cards.tip: Inclui aceitação do cliente e arquivamento.

cards.def: The activities to collect project records, analyze success, and archive information.
cards.ex: Administrative closure ensured all documents were filed.
cards.tip: Parte do encerramento.

cards.def: The process of finalizing open contracts and settling any claims.
cards.ex: Procurement closure included a final payment and release of liability.
cards.tip: Garante que todas as obrigações foram cumpridas.

cards.def: A document summarizing project performance, including outcomes and lessons.
cards.ex: The final report was presented to the steering committee.
cards.tip: Entregável obrigatório no encerramento.

cards.def: The process of transferring deliverables to the customer or operations.
cards.ex: The handover included training and documentation.
cards.tip: Pode ser faseada.

cards.def: A plan describing how the project's outputs will be transferred to operations.
cards.ex: The transition plan outlined support responsibilities after launch.
cards.tip: Garante continuidade.

cards.def: The process of ensuring that the project's benefits are achieved as planned.
cards.ex: Benefits realization was measured six months after project completion.
cards.tip: Pode ocorrer após o projeto.

cards.def: A systematic approach to improve the value of a project by optimizing costs and functions.
cards.ex: Value engineering suggested using a cheaper material without compromising quality.
cards.tip: Foco em função/custo.

cards.def: The total cost of ownership over the life of an asset, including acquisition, operation, and disposal.
cards.ex: Life cycle costing showed that a more expensive machine was cheaper in the long run.
cards.tip: Importante para decisões de investimento.

cards.def: The estimated cost of a product or service that is the target for development.
cards.ex: The target cost for the new feature was $50,000.
cards.tip: Usado em contratos de incentivo.

cards.def: An estimate of what a product or service should cost, used to evaluate seller proposals.
cards.ex: The should-cost estimate helped negotiate a fair price.
cards.tip: Baseado em análise detalhada.

cards.def: The process of gathering information about market conditions and capabilities.
cards.ex: Market research identified three potential suppliers.
cards.tip: Parte do planejamento de aquisições.

cards.def: A meeting with prospective sellers to ensure they have a clear understanding of the procurement.
cards.ex: The bidder conference clarified questions about the RFP.
cards.tip: Também chamado de contractor conference.

cards.def: A document from a seller offering to provide a product or service in response to a request.
cards.ex: The proposal included technical and commercial sections.
cards.tip: Avaliada conforme critérios.

cards.def: A statement of price for a product or service, often with terms.
cards.ex: The quote was valid for 30 days.
cards.tip: Menos detalhada que uma proposta.

cards.def: An offer by a seller to provide a product or service at a specified price.
cards.ex: The lowest bid was $1.2M.
cards.tip: Em leilões, pode ser competitivo.

cards.def: The decision to select a seller and enter into a contract.
cards.ex: The award was given to the vendor with the best technical score.
cards.tip: Formalizado por carta de adjudicação.

cards.def: A legally binding agreement between buyer and seller.
cards.ex: The contract specified deliverables, price, and terms.
cards.tip: Pode ser escrito ou verbal, mas escrito é preferível.

cards.def: The legal and commercial clauses in a contract.
cards.ex: Payment terms were net 30 days.
cards.tip: Devem ser claras para evitar disputas.

cards.def: A promise by the seller to fix defects for a specified period.
cards.ex: The software came with a 12-month warranty.
cards.tip: Pode ser incluída no contrato.

cards.def: Legal responsibility for something, such as damages.
cards.ex: The contract limited the seller's liability to the contract value.
cards.tip: Importante definir limites.

cards.def: An obligation to compensate for loss or damage.
cards.ex: The seller agreed to indemnify the buyer against third-party claims.
cards.tip: Comum em contratos de serviços.

cards.def: Unforeseeable circumstances that prevent someone from fulfilling a contract.
cards.ex: The contract included a force majeure clause for natural disasters.
cards.tip: Isenta as partes de responsabilidade.

cards.def: The ending of a contract before completion, either by agreement or for cause.
cards.ex: The project was terminated due to budget cuts.
cards.tip: Deve prever direitos e obrigações na rescisão.

cards.def: The finalization of a contract, including payment and release.
cards.ex: Close-out documents included a final invoice and release of claims.
cards.tip: Sinônimo de closure.

cards.def: A formal examination of project records, processes, or deliverables.
cards.ex: The audit confirmed compliance with the quality plan.
cards.tip: Pode ser interna ou externa.

cards.def: Adherence to laws, regulations, standards, or requirements.
cards.ex: The project ensured compliance with data protection laws.
cards.tip: Monitoramento contínuo.

cards.def: The framework of authority and control within which projects are managed.
cards.ex: Project governance included steering committee oversight.
cards.tip: Define papéis e responsabilidades.

cards.def: A group of senior stakeholders who provide guidance and approve key decisions.
cards.ex: The steering committee met quarterly to review progress.
cards.tip: Geralmente inclui o sponsor.

cards.def: Another term for steering committee, common in PRINCE2.
cards.ex: The project board included the executive, senior user, and senior supplier.
cards.tip: Responsável pela direção do projeto.

cards.def: The first meeting with the project team and stakeholders to align on objectives and plans.
cards.ex: The kick-off meeting introduced the team and reviewed the project charter.
cards.tip: Marca o início da execução.

cards.def: Regular meetings to review progress, issues, and next steps.
cards.ex: Weekly status meetings kept everyone informed.
cards.tip: Devem ser objetivas e focadas.
